title: Catamaran Charter Wedding in Puerto Banus: Costs and Tips
html:
A catamaran charter wedding in Puerto Banus pairs calm Costa del Sol water with a stable double-hull deck, leaving from the marina at Puerto Jose Banus. Through BoatHire24 a private day on a sailing catamaran here usually runs from around EUR 900 to EUR 2,500, depending on size and crew.

Where to set off in Puerto Banus

Most wedding charters cast off from the main marina at Puerto Jose Banus, the glossy harbour built in 1970 on the western edge of Marbella. From there a flat-water cruise can take in several genuinely lovely stretches of coast within an easy hour or two of sailing.

  • Puerto Jose Banus: the central departure marina, with berths, restaurants and parking close to the breakwater.
  • Playa de Nueva Andalucia: the broad sandy bay just east of the harbour mouth, calm in summer.
  • Marbella: the old town and seafront are roughly 7 nautical miles east along the shore.
  • Estepona: a quieter town to the west, around 13 nautical miles down the coast for a longer day.
  • San Pedro de Alcantara: the wide beach immediately west of Puerto Banus, handy for an anchored swim stop.
  • La Concha mountain: the dramatic ridge that frames every photo taken from the water here.

Which boat to rent in Puerto Banus for a wedding

Sailing catamarans suit weddings because the wide twin hulls stay level at anchor, so guests can move, eat and dance without the roll of a monohull. The choice below ranges from intimate to large-group celebrations, and you can compare hulls and dates on boat hire Puerto Banus before you commit.

  1. Sailing catamaran: best for the ceremony and reception itself, seating 12 to 20 guests in shade and sun, from EUR 1,200 a day.
  2. Luxury power catamaran: best for speed between coves with a large flybridge bar, up to 20 guests, from EUR 2,000 a day.
  3. Crewed motor yacht: best for a smaller, polished party with cabins, around 10 to 12 guests, from EUR 1,500 a day.
  4. Motorboat or RIB: best for the couple's photo run and quick guest transfers, 6 to 10 guests, from EUR 350 a half-day.
  5. Sunset cruiser: best for an evening toast under La Concha, 8 to 12 guests, from EUR 600 for the evening slot.

The Leopard 43 is a popular size for a mid-week ceremony, and you can read the spec on the Shashani listing to gauge cabin and deck space.

How much does it cost to rent a catamaran in Puerto Banus?

A private catamaran wedding day in Puerto Banus typically costs between EUR 900 and EUR 2,500, set by hull size, crew and season. Prices rise sharply in midsummer, so a spring or autumn date often buys more boat for the budget.

  • Shared catamaran tour with other guests: from about EUR 45 per person.
  • Small motorboat half-day for the couple: from roughly EUR 350 in EUR.
  • Catamaran full-day private charter: commonly EUR 1,200 to EUR 2,500.
  • Crewed yacht with skipper and host: often EUR 1,800 and above per day.

When is the best season for a Puerto Banus wedding charter?

High season runs June to September, with warm settled seas and the most reliable wedding-day weather. May and October are mild, less crowded and often better value for a private booking.

The dominant local breeze is the Levante, an easterly wind that can pick up the swell, while the gentler westerly Poniente usually keeps the bay flat. A skippered catamaran lets you and your guests relax entirely, and the operator confirms any licence requirements for your boat and date.

For a 2026 wedding, hold a backup time slot two hours later than your ceremony: a midsummer Levante often eases by late afternoon, giving calmer water for the toast and the photographs.

How does booking a wedding charter work?

You pick a date, party size and boat, send a request, and the operator confirms the crew, the route and any catering or decoration add-ons before payment. Most listings show capacity, included extras and cancellation terms up front, so a wedding booking carries no surprises on the day.

At a glance

  • Price band: EUR 900 to EUR 2,500 for a private day.
  • Peak months: June to September.
  • Trip length: full-day charters run 6 to 8 hours.
  • Capacity: typically 12 to 20 guests on a catamaran.
  • Currency: euro (EUR).
  • Departure: Puerto Jose Banus marina.

Questions about boat rental in Puerto Banus

Can we hold the actual ceremony on board?

Yes, many couples exchange vows at anchor in a sheltered bay, then cruise back for the reception; legal formalities are usually completed onshore beforehand, so the deck moment is the symbolic one your guests remember.

How many people fit on a wedding catamaran?

A typical sailing catamaran in Puerto Banus comfortably hosts 12 to 20 guests for a day charter, while larger power catamarans stretch a little higher, so confirm the certified passenger limit when you request your chosen boat.

Do we need to bring our own catering?

Not necessarily, because many operators arrange canapes, drinks and a cake through their own suppliers, though you can often bring your own provisions instead; agree the catering plan in writing when you confirm the date.

What happens if the weather turns?

Operators monitor the forecast and will propose a sheltered route or an alternative date if a strong Levante is due, since safety and a steady deck matter more than the original plan for a wedding day.

BoatHire24 is a worldwide marketplace where you can rent a boat in Puerto Banus or list your own, so couples and crews meet in one verified place. Begin your planning, compare catamarans for your wedding date, and reach out to operators directly through boathire24.com when you are ready to confirm.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Puerto Banus and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
